blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
If you are looking to play **Blind Man's Bluff** (also widely known as **Indian Poker** or forehead poker) on your Android device, there are a few excellent options available on the Google Play Store:

### 1. **Blindex - Blind Card Game**
* **Overview:** This is a highly polished, single-player psychological card game based entirely on the rules of Blind Man's Bluff. 
* **How it works:** You are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but your AI opponent can. Meanwhile, the AI’s card is displayed face-up in the center for you to see. You must read the AI’s betting patterns and facial expressions to guess your card's value.
* **Features:** 
  * Three AI difficulty levels (Easy, Normal, and Hard).
  * An "Expression Mind Game" mechanic where you can change your avatar's expressions (like smirking or sweating) to manipulate the AI's decisions.
  * 100% offline play with no Wi-Fi or account registration required.

### 2. **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **Overview:** A great option if you want to play the classic Blind Man's Bluff setup with other people. 
* **How it works:** Designed for local multiplayer, it allows you to play the game with friends and family. You predict your victory by looking only at your opponent's card, testing your psychological reading skills and bluffing abilities.
* **Features:**
  * Clean, straightforward interface.
  * Focuses on multiplayer party-style gameplay.

### 3. **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
* **Overview:** A lightweight, simple utility and simulation app designed to facilitate playing the physical card game with a group of people. 
* **How it works:** It acts as a digital dealer/card displayer for multiple players sitting together, making it easier to play the game in a party setting without needing a physical deck of cards.

forehead poker app Android.response:
If you are looking for a "forehead poker" app on Android, the term can refer to two different types of games: **Indian Poker / Blind Man's Bluff** (the classic card game where you hold a playing card to your forehead) or **Heads Up! / Charades** (the party game where you hold your phone to your forehead to guess words). 

The best real, existing apps on the Google Play Store for both styles of gameplay include:

### 1. Indian Poker & Blind Man's Bluff (Card Games)
These apps simulate the classic card game where you can see everyone else's card but your own, and you must bet based on their reactions.

*   **Indian Poker - Card Game** (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
    *   **Description:** This app is designed specifically to let you play the classic forehead card game with friends and family. You hold the device up, look at your opponents' cards, read their facial expressions, and use psychology to guess if your hidden card is the winning hand.
*   **Indian poker** (by Kanbe)
    *   **Description:** A straightforward multiplayer simulation app that lets you play Indian poker digitally. It is a lightweight, ad-supported option designed for quick local play with multiple people.
*   **Blindex — Blind Man's Bluff (Indian Poker)**
    *   **Description:** If you don't have a group to play with, this is a great offline, single-player version of the game. You hold your virtual card to your head, read the AI's "tells" and betting patterns, and try to bluff your way to victory across three difficulty levels.

---

### 2. "Heads Up!" Style Forehead Games (Party Word Games)
If you meant the popular party game where you hold the phone to your forehead and guess words or phrases based on your friends' clues, these are the top Android options:

*   **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
    *   **Description:** The official, wildly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. You select a deck (such as Blockbuster Movies, Animals, or custom categories), place the phone on your forehead facing your friends, and try to guess the words before the 60-second timer runs out. Tilting the phone down marks a correct answer, while tilting it up passes.
*   **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** (by Netflix, Inc.)
    *   **Description:** If you have an active Netflix subscription, you can download this version for free with no ads. It features exclusive decks based on popular Netflix shows like *Stranger Things*, *Squid Game*, and *Bridgerton*.
*   **Charades! Heads Up & Game Fun**
    *   **Description:** A highly-rated free alternative to the official game. It features offline play and a variety of categories like animals, professions, and fruits, allowing you to easily customize the number of cards per round.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
If you are looking for card games where you cannot see your own cards (often referred to as "Blind Man's Bluff," "Indian Poker," or cooperative games like "Hanabi"), several excellent Android apps on the Google Play Store fit this exact mechanic:

### 1. Blindex - Blind Card Game
* **What it is:** A single-player psychological card game based entirely on the classic **Blind Man's Bluff** (Indian Poker). 
* **How it works:** You are dealt a card face-down that you cannot see, but your AI opponent's card is shown face-up. You have to read the AI's "tells," expressions, and betting patterns to guess the value of your own card. You can call, raise, fold, or go all-in based on your psychological deduction. It features offline play and multiple AI difficulty levels.

### 2. Indian Poker - Card Game (by Wooah Tech Inc.)
* **What it is:** A dedicated mobile version of the classic party game **Indian Poker**.
* **How it works:** Designed to be played with friends and family, this app lets you play the "card-on-the-forehead" game digitally. You can only see your opponents' cards, and they can only see yours. The goal is to read their reactions, manage your betting, and bluff your way to victory without ever looking at your own hand.

### 3. Boardible - Board Games
* **What it is:** A companion and digital board game platform that features a fully playable version of **Hanabi**.
* **How it works:** *Hanabi* is an award-winning cooperative card game where players try to launch a perfect fireworks show. The core mechanic is that **you hold your cards facing away from you**—you can see everyone else's hand, but you cannot see your own. You must rely on limited, strategic clues from your teammates to figure out which cards to play or discard. 

### 4. Hanabi Tracker
* **What it is:** A highly-rated utility app for players of the physical *Hanabi* card game.
* **How it works:** If you already own the physical card game *Hanabi* but find it difficult to remember the clues your friends have given you (since you can't look at your cards), this app acts as a digital assistant. It lets you easily log and track the colors and numbers of the cards in your hand based on the clues you receive, freeing you up to focus on the game's strategy.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The type of game you are looking for is a charades-style party game, often referred to as a "heads up" or "headbands" game. In these games, you select a category, hold your phone to your forehead with the screen facing your friends, and try to guess the word on the screen based on their clues. 

The best and most popular apps for this on the Google Play Store include:

### 1. **Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros. International Enterprises)
This is the official, incredibly popular game popularized by Ellen DeGeneres. 
* **How it works:** You choose a deck (such as Blockbuster Movies, Animals, Accents, or Celebrities), hold the phone to your forehead, and guess the words as your friends shout clues. 
* **Controls:** Tilt your head/phone down when you get an answer right, or tilt it up to skip. It also has a hilarious feature that records video of your friends acting out the clues so you can watch it afterward.

### 2. **Heads Up! Netflix Edition** (by Netflix, Inc.)
If you have an active Netflix subscription, you can play this version completely free without ads or in-app purchases.
* **How it works:** It features the exact same forehead-guessing gameplay as the original but includes exclusive decks themed around popular Netflix shows and movies (like *Stranger Things*, *Squid Game*, and *Bridgerton*).

### 3. **Charades Guess Game — Guessly** (by Cosmoshark)
Formerly known as *ForeHeads*, this is one of the highest-rated alternative headband games on Android.
* **How it works:** It features over 48 unique decks and more than 6,500 challenges. It is highly customizable, has a clean interface, and offers a great mix of classic charades, trivia, and "Who Am I?" style guessing.

### 4. **Headbands: Charades Party Game** (by x64 Architecture)
A fantastic, lightweight, and free alternative to the official game.
* **How it works:** It features over 40 unique categories (including WWE, Disney, professions, and cartoon characters). It uses the same intuitive tilt-to-correct or tilt-to-skip mechanics and is perfect for quick, offline play during gatherings.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
Here are some of the best real, offline, single-player bluffing card games available on the Google Play Store for Android:

### 1. **Cheat! – Bluffing Card Game**
* **The Game:** A classic digital adaptation of the popular card game "Cheat" (also known as *Bluff*, *BS*, or *I Doubt It*). 
* **How it works offline:** You play face-down cards and declare their rank, trying to sneak lies past your opponents. It is fully playable offline against three smart CPU opponents. 
* **Key Features:** Includes strategic items like "Lie Counters" and "Bluff Shields" to add a unique twist to the classic rules, alongside daily missions and offline stat tracking.

### 2. **Bluff Card Game Offline** (by Vertex Games)
* **The Game:** Another excellent, dedicated offline version of the classic *I Doubt It* / *Cheat* card game.
* **How it works offline:** It requires absolutely no internet connection to play. You can choose between a quick 1v1 match against a single AI opponent or a full 4-player table with multiple AI players.
* **Key Features:** Smooth card animations, dynamic AI difficulty that adjusts to your playstyle, and a clean, distraction-free user interface.

### 3. **Liar's Cards - Ship of Deceit**
* **The Game:** A dark, pirate-themed bluffing card game heavily inspired by the popular PC game *Liar's Bar*.
* **How it works offline:** You play against AI pirate rivals on a cursed ship. Each player is dealt a hand from a small 20-card deck and must play cards matching a declared rank—or lie about it. 
* **Key Features:** If you get caught bluffing, you must face a tense "poisoned mug challenge" (a Russian roulette-style mechanic where you drink from mugs, one of which is poisoned).

### 4. **Texas Holdem Poker - Offline** (by Blue Wind)
* **The Game:** If you want a more traditional card game where bluffing is the core mechanic, offline Texas Hold'em is a great option.
* **How it works offline:** This app is designed specifically for offline play. You play against 8 different types of advanced computer AI personalities, allowing you to practice your poker face, betting strategies, and bluffs without needing an internet connection.
* **Key Features:** Completely free virtual chips, realistic AI behaviors, and detailed statistics to track how successful your bluffs actually are.

### 5. **Liar's Dice Online Multiplayer** (by YOAMB B.V.)
* **The Game:** While technically a dice-based game rather than a card game, *Liar's Dice* (also known as *Perudo* or *Dudo*) is one of the ultimate bluffing games in existence.
* **How it works offline:** Despite "Online" being in the title, the app features a robust **Offline Practice** mode. You can play single-player against highly intelligent AI opponents to practice bidding and calling out bluffs.
* **Key Features:** Beautiful 3D physics, multiple rule variants (like Calza and Wilds), and an interactive tutorial to help you master the art of the bluff.

best offline single player card game And.response:
These top-rated, single-player card games for Android are fully playable offline, feature no intrusive microtransactions, and offer deep, engaging gameplay:

### 1. **Balatro**
* **Genre:** Poker Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** *Balatro* is a massive hit that brings a hypnotic, poker-themed roguelike experience to mobile. The goal is to play illegal poker hands to score points and beat the "blinds". By buying and combining unique Joker cards, tarot cards, and planet cards, you can trigger massive, satisfying point multipliers. It is a premium game with zero ads or in-app purchases, making it perfect for endless offline play.

### 2. **Slay the Spire**
* **Genre:** Roguelike Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Widely considered the gold standard of the deckbuilding genre, *Slay the Spire* tasks you with climbing a procedurally generated tower. You choose from four distinct characters, each with their own unique card pools and playstyles. As you defeat enemies, you draft new cards and collect powerful relics to build a synergistic deck. It is incredibly deep, highly replayable, and runs flawlessly offline.

### 3. **Dawncaster: Deckbuilding RPG**
* **Genre:** Dark Fantasy RPG Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Designed specifically for mobile, *Dawncaster* is played in portrait mode, making it incredibly comfortable for one-handed play. It blends classic deckbuilding with deep RPG mechanics, allowing you to choose a class (like Warrior, Rogue, or Seeker) and build a deck around class-specific weapons and magic. It features over 900 hand-illustrated cards and is entirely offline.

### 4. **Night of the Full Moon**
* **Genre:** Dark Fairy Tale Deckbuilder
* **Why it’s great:** Based on the story of Little Red Riding Hood, this game features a gorgeous, dark-cartoon art style. You face off against various fairy-tale monsters in turn-based card combat. It is played in portrait mode, has a very gentle learning curve, and offers several different classes to master. The base game is free to play offline, with optional paid expansions for extra classes and storylines.

### 5. **Card Thief**
* **Genre:** Stealth Puzzle Card Game
* **Why it’s great:** If you want something different from traditional deckbuilders, *Card Thief* is a brilliant puzzle game played on a 3x3 grid of cards. You play as a thief sneaking through a deck of guards, torches, and traps. You must path your way through the grid to steal treasure and escape without running out of stealth points. It is tactical, quick to play, and highly rewarding.

### 6. **Ultimate Offline Card Games**
* **Genre:** Classic Card Game Compilation
* **Why it’s great:** If you prefer traditional card games over fantasy deckbuilders, this app is a must-have. It bundles classic games like Solitaire, Hearts, Spades, Rummy 500, Gin Rummy, and Euchre into a single, clean package. It features highly capable offline AI opponents, smooth animations, and requires absolutely no internet connection to play.

blind man's bluff card game app for Andr.response:
The Android app for **Blind Man's Bluff** (also known as **Indian Poker**) is called **Blindex** (specifically *Blindex - Blind Card Game*).

**Key details about Blindex:**
*   **Game Type:** It is a **single-player** psychological card game where you see your opponent's card but **never your own** [1][2].
*   **Offline Play:** The app runs **100% offline** with no internet connection required [1][2].
*   **Cost:** It is **100% free** to download and play, with **no ads** and **no in-app purchases** [1].
*   **Features:** You play against an AI dealer with **three difficulty levels** that have different bluffing styles and tells to read [1]. Points earned can be spent on cosmetic items like hairstyles, outfits, and card decks [1].
*   **Availability:** It is available worldwide on the **Google Play Store** [1][2].

**Google Play Store Link:**  
Search for "**Blindex - Blind Card Game**" or use the ID `com.blindex.app` [2].

*Note: Other apps named "Bluff" on Android (such as those by Floating Ninja Games or Oneiric Gaming) typically refer to the game "Cheat" (also known as BS or I Doubt It), where players try to discard cards by lying about their rank, which is a different game from Blind Man's Bluff [3][5][8].*

forehead poker app Android.response:
There is no Android app officially named **“Forehead Poker,”** but you are likely looking for **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** by Comunix Ltd., which features a unique **“forehold” mode** where you hold your phone up to your forehead to see your cards—mimicking Indian Poker or “forehead poker” style [1][2].

### Key App Recommendation:
- **App Name**: **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker**  
- **Developer**: Comunix Ltd.  
- **Available on**: Google Play Store (Android) [1]  
- **Forehead Feature**: You hold your phone/tablet up to your forehead to view your hand, just like in Indian Poker [2].  
- **Free to download**, with over 48,000 downloads in the last 30 days [7].  
- **Latest version**: 1.19.03, updated May 22, 2025 [7].  
- **Content Rating**: High Maturity (suitable for adults) [7].

### How to Get It:
1. Open the **Google Play Store** on your Android device.
2. Search for **“Poker Face Texas Holdem”**.
3. Install the app with ID `com.comunix.pokerface` [1].

If you were instead looking for a **party guessing game** (like *Heads Up!*) with poker themes, note that *Heads Up!* itself is not available on Android, but **Heads Up Charades!** is a close alternative [5]. However, for actual poker gameplay with the forehead mechanic, **Poker Face** is the correct and only known app matching your description.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
The card game you are describing is most likely **BS** (also known as **I Doubt It**, **Cheat**, or **Fibonacci**), where players hold cards they cannot see and must guess or bluff about their identity, but there is no single dedicated Android app specifically named for this mechanic that is widely recognized as *the* app for it.

However, the feature you want (cards visible to everyone *except* the player holding them) is a specific mode found in **virtual tabletop simulators** rather than a traditional card game. The best Android app to achieve this is:

### **Top Recommendation: Tabletop Simulator (via VirtualTableTop.io or similar)**
While the official *Tabletop Simulator* is primarily on Steam (PC), the web-based version **VirtualTableTop.io** (mentioned in search results) supports this exact "reverse hand" feature on mobile browsers, allowing cards to be visible to all other players but hidden from the owner [4][8].

If you need a dedicated **Android app** from the Google Play Store that supports this "blind hand" mechanic for party games:

| App Name | Why it fits |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Deck of Cards** (by *Card Game Studio* or similar) | Often includes party modes; search for "party card games" in the app description for "blind" or "secret" modes [7]. |
| **MiniCards — Card games** | Includes games like **Hearts**, **Spades**, and **Snap**; some of these or the "custom" mode may allow hidden hands via Bluetooth local play [7]. |
| **Card Game Simulator** | A flexible simulator where you can likely configure a "blind hand" game mode manually [5]. |

### How to find the exact game
Since "BS" is a party game often played with a physical deck, the most reliable way to play this on Android is to use a **multiplayer card game app with a "Custom" or "Party" mode**:

1.  Download **MiniCards** or **Group Card Games** from the Google Play Store [7].
2.  Look for a game mode called **"Blind"**, **"Secret"**, or **"Cheat"**.
3.  If the app allows **custom rules**, set the rule: *"Player's hand is hidden from themselves but visible to others."*

**Note:** Search results confirm that in the game **BS**, cards are *always* hidden unless called out, which matches your description of the game mechanics [3]. If you are looking for the *physical* game rules, it is simply called **BS** or **I Doubt It**. For the *app* experience, **VirtualTableTop.io** is the only source explicitly confirming the "Reverse" option to hide a card's identity from the player holding it [4].

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
The Android card game you’re describing is **Heads Up!**, where one player holds the phone to their forehead with the screen facing outward, and friends give clues to help them guess the word shown on the “card” [1][3].

### Key Features of **Heads Up!**:
- **How to play**: Choose a themed deck (e.g., animals, celebrities, accents), hold the phone to your forehead like a headband, and guess the word based on your friends’ clues before the 60-second timer runs out [1][4].
- **Guessing mechanic**: If you guess correctly, tilt the phone down to score a point; if not, tilt it up to pass [4][8].
- **Popularity**: The game gained widespread fame after being played by **Ellen DeGeneres** on her TV show [3].
- **Available on Google Play**: The official app is titled **Heads Up!** by Warner Bros. (ID: `com.wb.headsup`) [1].

### Similar Alternatives:
If you’re looking for other apps with the same forehead-holding mechanic:
- **Charades & Headbands Game** – A charades-style guessing game where you place the device on your forehead and guess based on friends’ miming or descriptions [2].
- **For Your Head** – Another forehead guessing game with trivia and word categories, also played by holding the phone to your forehead [5].

**Heads Up!** is the most popular and directly matches your description of holding a “card” (displayed on the phone) to your forehead while guessing from clues.

single player bluffing card game Android.response:
There is **no dedicated single-player "Bluff" (also known as Cheat or I Doubt It) card game** specifically optimized for offline Android play that is widely recognized as a top standalone app, as the classic Bluff game is inherently designed for **2+ players** to call out deception [1][6].

However, you can play **Bluff offline against intelligent AI bots** in the app **"Bluff Card Game"** (by O. Engines), which explicitly supports offline play with smart bots to simulate the bluffing experience [1][6]. If you are looking for other **single-player offline card games with strong bluffing or deception strategy elements**, consider these highly-rated alternatives available on Google Play:

| App Name | Why It Fits | Key Features |
|----------|-------------|--------------|
| **Card Thief** | Single-player bluffing/deception strategy | Offline, portrait mode, PvE, card-based stealth game where you bluff your way past guards [14] |
| **Poker World – Offline Texas Holdem** | Bluffing in poker context | Single-player offline poker where you battle and bluff through continents; no internet required [4] |
| **Ultimate Offline Card Games** | Multi-game hub including bluffing variants | Offline collection with Rummy, Spades, Hearts, and more; includes solo modes [3][12] |
| **Spades – Offline Card Games** | Trick-taking with AI opponents | Offline single-player Spades with high-level AI; similar strategic depth to bluffing games [5][9] |

**Best Recommendation:**  
For pure **bluffing deception** in a single-player offline format, **Card Thief** is the most unique and fitting choice—it’s a single-player card stealth game where bluffing is central to gameplay [14]. forehead poker app Android.response:
**Blindex** (available on Google Play): A free offline single-player Android app for Blind Man's Bluff / Indian Poker (also known as Forehead Poker). Place a card on your forehead, see opponents' cards, and bluff against an AI that bluffs back.[[1]](https://blindex.app/)

For standard Texas Hold'em poker apps on Android:  
- **Poker Face: Texas Holdem Poker** – Free online multiplayer Texas Hold'em with friends worldwide and millions of chips.

card game where you can't see your own c.response:
**Hanabi Tracker** (Google Play) – A utility app for tracking clues and information while playing the physical Hanabi card game, where you can't see your own cards but others can.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.rpalazzo.hanabitracker&hl=en_US)

The core game **Hanabi** (cooperative card game) perfectly matches your description—you hold cards facing outward so teammates see them but you don't—and it has digital implementations, though dedicated full-play Android apps on Google Play are limited (trackers and APKs exist instead). No other mainstream Android card games on the Play Store emphasize "can't see your own cards" mechanics as directly.

Android card game where you hold card to.response:
**Heads Up!** (by Warner Bros.) is the top Android app match. Players hold their phone to their forehead (like a headband), and teammates give clues to guess the word or phrase on the "card" before time runs out. It features various themed decks (celebrities, movies, etc.) and is a popular party game. Available on Google Play.[[1]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.wb.headsup&hl=en_US)

Other solid similar options on Google Play include:  
- **Charades & Headbands: Guess Up** (by Cosmicode) — Reverse charades-style gameplay where you place the phone on your forehead and guess based on acting/clues from friends, with many language and category options.[[2]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=pt.cosmicode.guessup&hl=en_US)  
- **Charades & Headbands Game** (by artGS) — Fun heads-up charades app with customizable themes; tilt your phone to mark correct or pass.[[3]](https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=net.artgamestudio.charadesapp&hl=en_US)  

These are all real, existing apps designed exactly for the forehead-holding guessing mechanic. Search for them directly in the Google Play Store.
